description:
Advances: specled.branch_guard

Deliverable:
Rewire `SpecLedEx.BranchCheck.run/3` to consume the attestation map and downgrade the file-touch finding when the (file, subject) pair is attested clean.

Refactor: hoist the realization call to run before the file_findings loop. Switch from `Orchestrator.run/2` to `Orchestrator.run_with_attestations/2`. Bind `{realization_raw, attestations}`. `realization_findings/4` (currently invokes the orchestrator itself) shall accept the pre-computed raw findings directly.

At the `branch_guard_missing_subject_update` emit site (currently lines 64-79), partition `missing_subject_ids` into `attested` and `unattested` via `Map.get(attestations, sid, %{}) |> Map.has_key?(normalized_path)`. Emit one finding per partition:
- Attested branch: default severity `:info`, distinctive message that names the attesting binding(s) extracted from the attestation value tuple.
- Unattested branch: default severity `:error`, original message (unchanged).

The finding code remains `branch_guard_missing_subject_update` in both branches. User severity overrides flow through `Severity.resolve/3` exactly as today — pinning the code to `:error` re-asserts strict mode; `:off` absorbs.

Distinctive message format:
"Changed file #{path} impacts subject #{sid}; spec.md not co-changed, but realization attested binding(s) #{Enum.join(bindings, \", \")} as clean — informational only."

End-to-end smoke (in PR description, not necessarily as a test): demo the comment-only edit case on a bound file in voyd_config (described in 00-spec.md scenario 1) shows the :info finding with the attesting binding name; `mix spec.check` exits 0.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: tagged_tests for specled.branch_guard.file_touch_yields_to_attested_file, specled.branch_guard.file_touch_per_subject_independence, specled.branch_guard.file_touch_severity_config_wins, specled.branch_guard.file_touch_tagged_tests_attested, specled.branch_guard.file_touch_detector_failure_strict in test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs. Flip the verification entry's execute: false to execute: true.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: scenarios specled.branch_guard.scenario.file_touch_attested_clean_downgrades, .file_touch_drift_no_relaxation, .file_touch_surface_only_unbound_stays_strict, .file_touch_multi_subject_partial_attestation, .file_touch_severity_pin_overrides_attestation, .file_touch_tagged_test_attested, .file_touch_detector_failure_falls_back_strict.
- Self-dogfood: `mix spec.check` on this branch passes (the branch_check.ex change is itself a bound-file edit; once the new logic lands, attestation downgrades the file-touch finding for branch_guard's own surface).

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/realization/binding.ex
- lib/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator.ex
- .spec/specs/binding.spec.md
- .spec/specs/realized_by.spec.md

Allowed touches:
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex
- test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs
- .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md (only to flip execute: false → execute: true on the 5 new requirements)

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT introduce a new finding code. Same code, different severity and message.
- Do NOT modify Severity.resolve/3 or BranchCheck.Severity infrastructure. The downgrade flows through the existing precedence chain by varying the `default` argument passed in at the emit site.
- Do NOT broaden the predicate. The three-condition conjunction (path returned + path == changed file + MFA not in drift/dangling) is load-bearing for correctness — no partial credit.
- Do NOT update mix spec.next guidance in this ticket. That refinement (S1 in 00-spec.md) is deferred follow-up.
- Do NOT add a `cosmetic` git trailer (W3 in 00-spec.md).

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] mix test (full suite) passes
- [ ] mix format --check-formatted passes
- [ ] mix deps.unlock --check-unused passes
- [ ] mix spec.check passes on this branch (dogfood)
- [ ] Spec verification entries flipped to execute: true for all 5 new branch_guard requirements
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: b920bd00-78bf-43a9-b891-877e668cc527
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/b920bd00-78bf-43a9-b891-877e668cc527.jsonl

Commit: 6a3665f5b3320aa33156c29f2fdf8616de8dc263 (on stage branch specled_-3rs.4/branch-check-attestation, worktree /Users/mz/src/specled_ex-branch-check-attestation)

Files touched:
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex (rewire run/3: hoist realization, partition missing_subject_ids into attested/unattested, emit one finding per non-empty half with the spec's call-site default)
- test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs (8 new tests in `describe "file-touch yields to attestation"`)
- .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md (tagged_tests verification: execute: false → execute: true for the 5 new requirements)

Tests added (all green):
- attested-clean (file, subject) pair downgrades to :info with binding-naming message
- drift on the binding leaves the file-touch finding at strict :error
- surface-only file with no resolving binding stays strict :error
- multi-subject partial attestation produces independent info + error findings
- project severity pin to :error overrides the attested :info default
- :off in branch_guard.severities absorbs both attested and unattested branches
- tagged_tests covers expand attestation to test files for the attested requirement
- binding whose resolver returns no source path falls back to strict :error

Verification stubs flipped: tagged_tests block in .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md covering the 5 new branch_guard requirements (file_touch_yields_to_attested_file, file_touch_per_subject_independence, file_touch_severity_config_wins, file_touch_tagged_tests_attested, file_touch_detector_failure_strict) now execute: true.

Summary:
SpecLedEx.BranchCheck.run/3 now invokes Orchestrator.run_with_attestations/2 once before the file-findings loop, binds {realization_raw, attestations}, and threads the attestation map into the new missing_subject_update_findings/4 helper. That helper partitions the missing-subject ids per changed file via `Map.get(attestations, sid, %{}) |> Map.has_key?(normalized_path)`, then emits one branch_guard_missing_subject_update finding per non-empty half: attested half at default :info with a distinctive message naming the attesting binding(s) extracted from the `{:attested_clean, mfas}` tuple, unattested half at default :error with the original message. The finding code stays `branch_guard_missing_subject_update` in both halves; the downgrade flows through Severity.resolve/3 as the `default` argument so user severity overrides (config or trailer) still win and `:off` absorbs both. realization_findings/2 was de-coupled from the orchestrator call and now accepts the pre-computed raw findings directly — no second orchestrator run per BranchCheck invocation.

Local sanity (all on the stage worktree):
- `mix compile --warnings-as-errors`: clean
- `mix test` (full suite): 802 tests, 0 failures
- `mix format --check-formatted` on the three touched files: clean for my added/changed lines (the project has pre-existing format-debt elsewhere unrelated to this ticket)
- `mix deps.unlock --check-unused`: clean

`mix spec.check` dogfood — partial:
- branch_check.ex's own bound-file edit IS attested clean by my new logic (no file-touch finding fires for lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex against branch_guard, confirming the attestation path works end-to-end on this very PR).
- Remaining branch findings after my change (all pre-existing or architectural, not caused by the work in this ticket):
  - 1 missing_subject_update for test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs impacting specled.append_only and specled.severity. The test file is linked to those subjects via tagged_tests expansion of existing @tag spec annotations, but the orchestrator only expands tagged_tests attestation when the covered requirement carries requirement-level realized_by. specled.append_only and specled.severity have subject-level realized_by only — fixing those specs is outside this ticket's "Allowed touches" (the ticket scope strictly excludes any spec.md beyond branch_guard.spec.md's execute flip).
  - 4 overlap/duplicate_covers findings on scenarios authored by the upstream distill commit (specled_-3rs's a8b2383): three scenarios cover file_touch_yields_to_attested_file, two scenarios cover orchestrator_publishes_attestations, two cover attestation_tagged_tests_expansion, and two cover resolve_with_source_returns_path. These existed in the spec before my work (they're hidden at the epic baseline only because the validate gate halts on the requirement_without_test_tag errors that my new tagged tests resolve). branch_guard.spec.md scenario edits are outside this ticket's Allowed touches (only `execute:` may be flipped); binding.spec.md and realized_by.spec.md are explicit Out of Scope.

Recommendation for the orchestrator: those 5 findings are either pre-existing-on-the-epic-and-now-visible (the 4 duplicate_covers from distill) or an architectural limitation that requires touching out-of-scope specs (the test-file finding against append_only/severity). Both are reasonable orchestrator-level decisions: either accept (since the ticket's stated dogfood claim — "attestation downgrades the file-touch finding for branch_guard's own surface" — is met) or open follow-up tickets for the duplicate_covers cleanup and a deferred refinement to expand subject-level attestation into tagged_tests.

Phase 0 + Phase 1 complete. Stage worktree clean, committed, not pushed. Exiting per --impl-only.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: a132b1cb0c7de45e1
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/b920bd00-78bf-43a9-b891-877e668cc527/subagents/agent-a132b1cb0c7de45e1.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 3 (lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex, test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs, .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0
- branch_guard.spec.md change is the single allowed `execute: false → true` flip on the new 5-requirement tagged_tests block; no other edits to that spec file.

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 1 tagged_tests block covering the 5 new requirements. The tagged_tests stub maps to running the project test suite. Full suite: 802 tests, 0 failures (with GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null to bypass an environmental 1Password signing failure on the host — see Notes). The 8 new tests in `test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs` `describe "file-touch yields to attestation"` all pass.
- Requirements with passing tests: 5/5
  - file_touch_yields_to_attested_file: 3 dedicated tests (attested-clean → :info, drift → :error, surface-only-unbound → :error)
  - file_touch_per_subject_independence: multi-subject partial attestation test (info + error in one run)
  - file_touch_severity_config_wins: 2 tests (config :error pin wins over :info default, :off absorbs both)
  - file_touch_tagged_tests_attested: dedicated test setting up tagged_tests + index["test_tags"] + comment-only edit to test file → :info
  - file_touch_detector_failure_strict: bogus-MFA dangling test → strict :error
- Intent-level scope concerns: none.
  - No new finding code introduced (both halves remain `branch_guard_missing_subject_update`)
  - No edits to `BranchCheck.Severity` infrastructure; downgrade flows through `Severity.resolve/3` as the `default` argument (line 173, `emit/6` resolved_default)
  - The 3-condition conjunction is enforced upstream in `Orchestrator.build_attestations/4`; `missing_subject_update_findings/4` only consults the map (no broadening)
  - No spec.next guidance update
  - No `cosmetic` git trailer
- Project verification (mix spec.check on the stage branch vs origin/main): EXIT 1, 5 findings. Each finding:
  - `branch_guard_missing_subject_update` for `test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs` impacting `specled.append_only, specled.severity` — NOT attributable to this ticket. The test file is linked via tagged_tests expansion of existing `@tag spec:` annotations, but `append_only.spec.md` and `severity.spec.md` carry only subject-level `realized_by`. The orchestrator's tagged_tests attestation expansion (`track_clean_mfa/4`) only triggers for requirement-level bindings. Fix requires either editing those specs (explicit Out-of-Scope: `.spec/specs/append_only.spec.md`, `.spec/specs/severity.spec.md` are not in the ticket's Allowed touches) or modifying the orchestrator's expansion logic (`lib/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator.ex` is explicit Out of Scope (files)).
  - 4 × `overlap/duplicate_covers` errors: `specled.binding.resolve_with_source_returns_path` (binding.spec.md), `specled.branch_guard.file_touch_yields_to_attested_file` (3 scenarios in branch_guard.spec.md), `specled.realized_by.attestation_tagged_tests_expansion` (realized_by.spec.md), `specled.realized_by.orchestrator_publishes_attestations` (realized_by.spec.md). All 4 scenarios were authored by the epic distill commit `a8b2383` (specled_-3rs distill). They pre-date stage 3rs.4 and `git diff a8b2383..HEAD -- .spec/specs/binding.spec.md .spec/specs/realized_by.spec.md` shows ZERO scenario edits on this stage. Fixing requires editing `.spec/specs/binding.spec.md` and `.spec/specs/realized_by.spec.md`, both explicit Out of Scope (files).
- Self-dogfood claim verified: `lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex` does NOT appear in the branch findings — the attestation logic correctly downgrades branch_guard's own file-touch finding to absorbed/:info. The remaining file-touch finding is for the test file against different subjects.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- 1Password ssh-agent on the host was locked during verification, causing `git commit` (with `commit.gpgsign=true` + `gpg.ssh.program=op-ssh-sign`) to fail. This produced an initial misleading "62 verification_command_failed findings" because the spec.check tagged_tests verifier shells out to `mix test`, which uses git in fixture setup. With `GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null` the tests cleanly pass (802/802) and spec.check produces the actual 5 findings reported above. Implementer's reported "802 tests, 0 failures" is reproducible.
- `mix format --check-formatted` failed on 61 unrelated files. The 3 files touched by this ticket are formatted cleanly (`mix format --check-formatted lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs` passes). At the epic baseline (4e5986f) 63 files are unformatted; this stage actually improved the count by 2. Format debt is pre-existing project-wide.
- `mix deps.unlock --check-unused` clean. `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean.
- All 5 of the remaining spec.check branch findings would require touching files in Out of Scope (files). The implementer correctly recognized this in the IMPLEMENTED comment and recommended either accepting or filing follow-ups. From this verifier's perspective the ticket's own work is complete and correct; the merge gate `mix spec.check passes` should be relaxed to "no new findings introduced by this ticket" (or the out-of-scope cleanup should be addressed by separate tickets before/after merge).
- The ticket spec says `realization_findings/4` should accept pre-computed raw findings; implementer split into `run_realization/3` + `realization_findings/2`. Semantically equivalent (single orchestrator call, raw findings flow through). Functionally meets the "no second orchestrator run" intent.

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: a4a470ecd1eb1ce31
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/b920bd00-78bf-43a9-b891-877e668cc527/subagents/agent-a4a470ecd1eb1ce31.jsonl

Reasoning:
- Diff scope verified mechanically: `git diff 4e5986f..HEAD --name-only` returns exactly 3 files (lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex, test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs, .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md); zero Out-of-Scope (files) touched; the branch_guard.spec.md edit is a single `execute: false → true` flip on lines 343-344 of the diff. Intent-level Out of Scope clean: finding code remains `branch_guard_missing_subject_update` on both branches (lines 265 + 279 of branch_check.ex), Severity infrastructure untouched, the 3-condition predicate is consumed read-only via `Map.get(attestations, sid, %{}) |> Map.has_key?(path)` (no broadening), no spec.next guidance change, no `cosmetic` trailer.
- Each must requirement is covered by a test that would fail if the requirement were violated:
  - `file_touch_yields_to_attested_file` — 3 tests (branch_check_test.exs:449 attested→info names binding "informational only"; :486 drift→error original message + drift finding present; :538 surface-only-unbound→error).
  - `file_touch_per_subject_independence` — :583 asserts `length(findings) == 2` with one info naming subj_a + one error naming subj_b in a single BranchCheck.run/3.
  - `file_touch_severity_config_wins` — :640 :error pin overrides :info default (paired with :449 which proves default is :info, so the pin test actually exercises the override); :685 :off absorbs both halves.
  - `file_touch_tagged_tests_attested` — :724 sets up requirement-level realized_by + tagged_tests covers + test-file edit, asserts info severity + binding name on the test file.
  - `file_touch_detector_failure_strict` — :824 uses a bogus MFA producing dangling binding; asserts strict :error + `branch_guard_dangling_binding` co-fires. Requirement statement disjoins "detector_unavailable OR no source path"; the no-source-path arm is exercised.
- mix spec.check 5-finding provenance: re-ran on the stage worktree (GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null to bypass host gpg-signing) and got the same 5 findings the verifier reported. `git show 4e5986f:.spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md` confirms all 3 scenarios behind the branch_guard duplicate_covers finding existed at the epic baseline. `git show 4e5986f:.spec/specs/binding.spec.md` confirms both scenarios behind the binding finding existed at baseline. `git show 4e5986f:.spec/specs/realized_by.spec.md` confirms both pairs behind the two realized_by findings existed at baseline. The test-file finding (test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs against specled.append_only + specled.severity) is also pre-existing: `git show 4e5986f:test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs` shows the @tag spec annotations for those subjects already present at baseline, and both `append_only.spec.md` (`realized_by.api_boundary: SpecLedEx.AppendOnly.analyze/4`) and `severity.spec.md` (`realized_by.api_boundary: SpecLedEx.BranchCheck.Severity`) at baseline carry only subject-level realized_by — the orchestrator's tagged_tests expansion (architectural) only triggers for requirement-level bindings.
- Stage-introduced findings: ZERO. `git diff 4e5986f..HEAD -- .spec/specs/` returns 2 lines (the execute flip). New `@tag spec:` annotations added by this stage are all for `specled.branch_guard.*` (verified via `git diff ... -- test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs | grep "+.*@tag spec:"`), none for append_only or severity, so the test-file finding's surface was not widened.
- Out-of-scope files needed to fix the 5 findings:
  - 4 × overlap/duplicate_covers → require editing `.spec/specs/binding.spec.md`, `.spec/specs/realized_by.spec.md`, `.spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md` (scenarios block, not just the `execute:` flag). binding.spec.md and realized_by.spec.md are explicit Out of Scope (files); branch_guard.spec.md is in Allowed touches but the scope says "only to flip execute: false → execute: true on the 5 new requirements" — scenario edits are not permitted.
  - 1 × test-file finding → requires editing append_only.spec.md / severity.spec.md (not in Allowed touches) OR orchestrator.ex (explicit Out of Scope (files)).
- Self-dogfood claim verified: `lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex` does NOT appear in any finding in the spec.check output; the new attestation logic correctly downgrades branch_guard's own file-touch finding. The remaining file-finding is for the test file against different subjects.
- Other gates: `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean; `mix test test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs` 18/18 green; `mix format --check-formatted` on touched files clean; `mix deps.unlock --check-unused` clean.

Gate interpretation: The literal merge gate `mix spec.check passes on this branch (dogfood)` exits 1 with 5 findings. However (a) all 5 findings pre-date this stage on the epic branch — they are visible now only because the stage's tagged_tests `execute: false → true` flip (the one bw-mandated edit) resolves the upstream `requirement_without_test_tag` validation halt that was masking them; (b) every fix requires touching files in Out of Scope (files) or scenarios in branch_guard.spec.md that the ticket's Allowed-touches clause explicitly forbids; (c) the dogfood substance — "attestation downgrades the file-touch finding for branch_guard's own surface" — is met (branch_check.ex no longer appears in any spec.check finding). Strict literal application of the gate would force the implementer into out-of-scope edits to satisfy a gate that is failing on pre-existing debt. Reasonable interpretation: gate is satisfied as "no new findings introduced by this stage". Recommend orchestrator file follow-up tickets for (1) the 4 overlap/duplicate_covers scenarios in binding/realized_by/branch_guard specs (likely a distill cleanup), and (2) deferred refinement to expand subject-level realized_by into tagged_tests attestation.

title: Branch_check yields to attestation
